visits.note: Patient presents with chief complaint of fatigue and increased thirst. Physical exam reveals obesity, BP 140/90, and 1+ pitting edema in bilateral lower extremities. Initial labs show HbA1c 7.8, BP_systolic 140, eGFR 65, and LDL 145. Diagnoses include Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Hyperlipidemia. Medications initiated: Metformin 500mg BID, Lisinopril 10mg QD, and Atorvastatin 20mg QD. Symptoms include polyuria, polydipsia, and fatigue.

Follow-up visit after 6 months of medication therapy. Patient reports improved energy levels but increased urination at night. BP 130/85, and no new edema noted. Labs show HbA1c 7.2, BP_systolic 130, eGFR 70, and LDL 135. Medications adjusted: Metformin increased to 1000mg BID, Lisinopril to 20mg QD, and Atorvastatin to 40mg QD. Diagnoses remain unchanged, but symptoms now include nocturia.

Patient reports worsening symptoms: increased urination, fatigue, and shortness of breath. BP 150/95, and 2+ pitting edema in bilateral lower extremities. Labs show HbA1c 8.1, BP_systolic 150, eGFR 55, and LDL 155. New diagnoses include Chronic Kidney Disease. Medications adjusted: Metformin increased to 1500mg BID, Lisinopril to 30mg QD, and Atorvastatin to 80mg QD. Symptoms now include orthopnea.

Patient reports continued worsening of symptoms: shortness of breath, orthopnea, and decreased urine output. BP 160/100, and 3+ pitting edema in bilateral lower extremities. Labs show HbA1c 8.5, BP_systolic 160, eGFR 45, and LDL 165. Medications adjusted: Metformin increased to 2000mg BID, Lisinopril to 40mg QD, and Atorvastatin to 100mg QD. New diagnoses include Coronary Artery Disease. Symptoms now include decreased urine output.
